target.title: [codex] Finalize RuntimePlan embedded-runner cleanup stack
target.body:
## Summary

This is the consolidated maintainer-facing RuntimePlan embedded-runner cleanup package for [RFC #72072](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/72072). It absorbs and supersedes [#72276](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/72276), so reviewers only need to track this PR going forward.

**Current review status:** #73767 is now the single authoritative surface for the RuntimePlan finalization stack. #72276 is preserved for audit history only and is being closed as superseded to avoid double-maintaining stacked CI and review comments.

**Scale:** against current `origin/main`, this consolidated PR is **92 files, +5866/-1419**. The branch is intentionally commit-separated so maintainers can request splits or cherry-pick by domain if needed.

**Maintainer tags:** @steipete @pashpashpash

## Why This Exists

#71004 established the contract-first Pi/Codex RuntimePlan boundary and was implemented by #71096 and #71722. #72072 tracks the post-implementation cleanup: make the RuntimePlan architecture maintainable by moving from “policy exists” to “policy is inspectable, testable, and named correctly.”

The previously separate #72276 structural cleanup base has been merged into this branch. This PR now carries both the structural cleanup base and the remaining finalization work:

- Extract model/auth planning out of embedded run orchestration.
- Extract abortable attempt stream-loop helpers out of the monolithic attempt path.
- Make neutral `embedded-runner` imports/docs canonical while preserving Pi compatibility aliases.
- Add native bundled Codex Harness V2 lifecycle support.
- Expose a public, same-plugin-ownership-checked Harness V2 factory API.
- Keep WebSocket pooling conservative and prove failed assistant turns are never pooled.
- Regenerate plugin SDK API baseline and update handoff docs/smoke instructions.

## Consolidated Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  Main["origin/main"] --> FinalStack["#73767 consolidated RuntimePlan cleanup package"]
  Superseded["#72276 structural split draft (superseded)"] -. "absorbed with traceability" .-> FinalStack

  FinalStack --> ModelAuth["model-auth-plan.ts extraction"]
  FinalStack --> StreamLoop["attempt-stream-loop.ts extraction"]
  FinalStack --> Naming["neutral embedded-runner imports + guard"]
  FinalStack --> Docs["smoke + finalization docs"]
  FinalStack --> CodexV2["native Codex Harness V2 factory"]
  FinalStack --> PublicV2["public Harness V2 plugin API"]
  FinalStack --> WsPool["WS pooling gate regression tests"]
  FinalStack --> PackageRename["canonical embedded-runner barrels"]
  FinalStack --> Hardening["adversarial hardening fixes"]
```

## Runtime Ownership After This Stack

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  UserTurn["User turn"] --> RuntimePlan["AgentRuntimePlan"]

  RuntimePlan --> Auth["Model/auth plan"]
  RuntimePlan --> Prompt["Prompt + overlays"]
  RuntimePlan --> Tools["Tools + schema policy"]
  RuntimePlan --> Transport["Transport params + WS pooling gate"]
  RuntimePlan --> Transcript["Transcript repair"]
  RuntimePlan --> Delivery["Delivery + NO_REPLY"]
  RuntimePlan --> Outcome["Outcome classification"]

  Auth --> EmbeddedRun["Embedded runner orchestration"]
  Prompt --> Attempt["Attempt setup + stream-loop helpers"]
  Tools --> Attempt
  Transport --> Attempt
  Transcript --> Attempt

  EmbeddedRun --> Pi["Pi runtime adapter"]
  EmbeddedRun --> Codex["Codex app-server adapter"]

  Pi --> HarnessV2["Harness V2 lifecycle"]
  Codex --> HarnessV2
  HarnessV2 --> Events["resolvedRef + terminal events"]
  Delivery --> Events
  Outcome --> Events
```

## Public Harness V2 Safety Model

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant Plugin as Plugin register(api)
  participant Registry as Plugin registry
  participant V1 as AgentHarness V1 registry
  participant V2 as Native Harness V2 registry
  participant Selection as Harness selection

  Plugin->>Registry: registerAgentHarness(id)
  Registry->>V1: record plugin-owned V1 harness id
  Plugin->>Registry: registerAgentHarnessV2Factory(id, factory)
  Registry->>Registry: verify same plugin already owns V1 id
  alt same-plugin owner
    Registry->>V2: install plugin-owned V2 factory
    Selection->>V2: prefer native V2 factory
  else missing / another plugin's id
    Registry-->>Plugin: diagnostic error, no V2 factory installed
    Selection->>V1: use V1 adapter fallback
  end
```

## Commit Stack Map

| Commit | Domain | What changed | Revert / split note |
|---|---|---|---|
| `7f50ad884d` | Model/auth planning | Adds `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/model-auth-plan.ts` and extracts model resolution, harness choice, auth-store setup, auth profile candidate ordering, plugin-owned transport detection, and no-leakage auth setup out of `run.ts`. | Behavior-preserving extraction; can split as “model-auth-plan extraction.” |
| `98d620d04c` | Attempt stream-loop helpers | Adds `attempt-stream-loop.ts` and extracts abort reason helpers, timeout abort reason, abortable wrapper, and prompt submission flow. | Helper extraction; does not change stop-reason or recovery policy. |
| `5b25fbd4af` | Neutral imports | Migrates safe hot-path imports to `embedded-runner` and adds a warning-only deprecated import guard. | Reversible without changing runtime behavior; guard remains warning-only. |
| `fbef56129c` | Smoke docs | Adds `runtime-plan-final-stack-smoke-handoff.md` and updates finalization docs. | Docs-only. |
| `d7f6ec7ca2` | Codex native V2 | Adds native bundled Codex Harness V2 factory while preserving app-server lifecycle ownership. | Internal additive factory; V1 remains supported. |
| `7d35fdef59` | Public Harness V2 API | Adds plugin-facing `registerAgentHarnessV2Factory(...)` and keeps V1 registration compatible. | Public additive API; V1 plugins still work. |
| `c25369f026` | WS pooling gates | Locks WS pooling gates and tests conservative behavior. | Test/policy hardening; pooling remains opt-in. |
| `211c7e861c` | Embedded-runner naming | Adds deprecated-import baseline, JSDoc deprecations, docs, and neutral public barrels. | Compatibility aliases remain. |
| `31d29c7745` | Extraction lint | Removes unnecessary type assertion from model-auth extraction. | Foldable into `7f50ad884d` if maintainers prefer. |
| `2a5b4e8bef` | Adversarial hardening | Fixes verified P1/P2/P3 issues from read-only adversarial review: V2 ownership hijack, stale docs, API baseline, sparse attempt normalization, WS error-turn pooling, Codex V2 classification parity, RuntimePlan retry input forwarding, and builtin V2 factory restore safety. | Final corrective batch; can be reviewed independently against the adversarial findings table below. |
| `3c6eb5020d` | Harness V2 owner scoping | Scopes native Harness V2 factories by harness id plus plugin owner, updates public plugin loader ownership checks, and adds plugin/core collision regressions. | Review-thread fix for the #72276/#73767 V2 factory collision finding; belongs with public Harness V2 API if split. |
| `6496bc8c1c` | Main refresh | Merges current `origin/main` into the consolidated branch and preserves the Harness V2 owner-scoped loader tests through the conflict. | Merge/update only; no standalone product behavior beyond current main. |

## File Map

| Area | Primary files |
|---|---|
| Model/auth extraction | `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/model-auth-plan.ts`, `model-auth-plan.test.ts`, `run.ts`, `runtime-plan-factory.ts`, `runtime-plan-factory.test.ts` |
| Attempt stream-loop helper extraction | `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt-stream-loop.ts`, `attempt-stream-loop.test.ts`, `run/attempt.ts` |
| Terminal / incomplete-turn hardening | `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts`, `run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`, `run.overflow-compaction.harness.ts` |
| WS pooling safety | `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts`, `run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `src/agents/openai-ws-stream.test.ts` |
| Harness V2 internals | `src/agents/harness/v2.ts`, `v2.test.ts`, `builtin-pi.ts`, `selection.ts`, `selection.test.ts` |
| Codex native Harness V2 | `extensions/codex/harness.ts`, `harness-v2.test.ts`, `index.ts`, `index.test.ts` |
| Public plugin API | `src/plugins/types.ts`, `api-builder.ts`, `registry.ts`, `loader.ts`, `loader.test.ts`, `captured-registration.ts`, `registry-types.ts`, `plugin-sdk/*`, `docs/plugins/sdk-agent-harness.md`, `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` |
| Neutral embedded-runner naming | `src/agents/embedded-runner.ts`, `embedded-runner/index.ts`, `pi-embedded-runner.ts`, `pi-embedded-runner/index.ts`, `pi-embedded.ts`, `aliases.test.ts`, `scripts/check-embedded-runner-imports.mjs`, `scripts/check-embedded-runner-imports-baseline.json`, `docs/pi.md` |
| Maintainer handoff docs | `docs/refactor/runtime-plan-final-stack-smoke-handoff.md`, `docs/refactor/runtime-plan-finalization-complete.md` |

## What Changed

- `run.ts` now delegates model/auth setup to a focused `model-auth-plan.ts` helper instead of keeping that policy in the orchestration body.
- `attempt.ts` now delegates prompt submission / abort wrapper logic to `attempt-stream-loop.ts` while keeping recovery state close to the main loop.
- Safe core call sites use neutral `embedded-runner` public barrels instead of historical Pi-named barrels.
- The deprecated import guard records a current baseline and reports only newly introduced `pi-embedded-runner` imports.
- Bundled Codex exposes a native internal Harness V2 factory while preserving V1 app-server behavior.
- Plugins can register native Harness V2 factories only for harness ids they already registered in the same plugin load, preventing cross-plugin hijacks.
- WS session pooling remains conservative and now explicitly refuses pooling after assistant error turns.
- RuntimePlan retry input forwarding preserves `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, `modelRun`, and `promptMode`.
- Sparse harness result arrays are normalized before terminal payload shaping so malformed adapter results do not crash downstream result code.

## What Did Not Change

- This PR does **not** remove public V1 `AgentHarness` registration.
- This PR does **not** require third-party plugins to implement Harness V2.
- This PR does **not** remove Pi-named compatibility exports or paths.
- This PR does **not** make WS pooling default-on.
- This PR does **not** resume #70743/#70772 prototype churn.
- This PR does **not** change Codex ownership of app-server/model-loop lifecycle; OpenClaw owns shared runtime policy.
- This PR does **not** claim live GPT-5.4 smoke has been run in CI; the smoke checklist is documented for operator environments with live credentials.

## Adversarial Review Pass

Six read-only high-threat review agents were run against this stack before finalizing this description. No P0 issues were found. Verified P1/P2/P3 findings were fixed in `2a5b4e8bef`.

## Validation

All commands below were run locally from `/Users/lume/repos/openclaw-2` after the final hardening changes.

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| `pnpm check:architecture` | ✅ 0 runtime value cycles, 0 madge cycles, deprecated internal config guard passed |
| `pnpm check:test-types` | ✅ core + extensions test typecheck passed |
| `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` | ✅ baseline matches generated public plugin SDK API |
| `git diff --check` | ✅ clean |
| `oxlint` on changed TS files in the hardening batch | ✅ 0 warnings / 0 errors |
| Focused agents suite: `runtime-plan-factory`, `run.incomplete-turn`, `attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine`, `harness/v2` | ✅ 4 files, 130 tests passed |
| Focused Codex suite: `extensions/codex/harness-v2.test.ts`, `extensions/codex/index.test.ts` | ✅ 2 files, 7 tests passed |
| Focused plugin loader ownership tests | ✅ 4 tests passed, 129 skipped by filter |
| Focused WS assistant-error pooling regression | ✅ passed |
| `pnpm check:changed` | ✅ passed |

Notes:

- For targeted file selection, direct `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config ... <full path>` was used. Some `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ... <relative shorthand>` invocations in this checkout matched no files and exited code 0, so the docs now show direct full-path vitest commands for maintainers.
- CI should now be evaluated on #73767 only. #72276 is superseded and no longer the merge target.

## Live GPT-5.4 Smoke Checklist

The live matrix requires operator credentials and is documented in `docs/refactor/runtime-plan-final-stack-smoke-handoff.md`.

| Route | Required smoke evidence |
|---|---|
| `openai/*` GPT-5.4 | Meaningful response, tool call, auth profile routing, schema normalization, fallback classification, resolvedRef observability. |
| `openai-codex/*` GPT-5.4 | Same plus OpenAI-Codex auth profile forwarding and Responses-family transport params. |
| `codex/*` GPT-5.4 | Same plus Codex app-server Harness V2 lifecycle and OpenClaw-owned tool hook behavior. |
| `codex-cli/*` GPT-5.4 | Same plus CLI auth profile aliasing and no auth leakage into unrelated CLI providers. |

## Rollback / Split Guidance

This PR is intentionally commit-separated. If reviewers ask for smaller PRs, split in this order:

1. `7f50ad884d` + `31d29c7745` portions + relevant `2a5b4e8bef` runtime-plan forwarding fixes: model/auth extraction.
2. `98d620d04c` + relevant `2a5b4e8bef` attempt cleanup fixes: attempt stream-loop helper extraction.
3. `5b25fbd4af` + `211c7e861c`: neutral embedded-runner imports / canonical barrels / import guard.
4. `d7f6ec7ca2`: bundled Codex native Harness V2.
5. `7d35fdef59` + same-plugin ownership fixes from `2a5b4e8bef`: public Harness V2 API.
6. `c25369f026` + WS assistant-error fixes from `2a5b4e8bef`: WS pooling gate tests and cleanup safety.
7. `fbef56129c` + docs updates from `2a5b4e8bef`: docs/smoke handoff.

## Deferred / Out Of Scope

- Run live GPT-5.4 smoke in an operator environment with real credentials.
- Decide whether WS pooling should become default-on in a later release.
- Decide when Pi-named compatibility exports can move from warning/deprecated to removal.
- Decide whether the deprecated import guard should become hard enforcement after compatibility migration.

target.title: Errors: rewrite 403 HTML auth pages

## Summary

Fix incorrect user-facing error reporting when provider requests return `403` HTML auth/challenge pages.

Previously these failures could be surfaced as transport or rate-limit style errors, which sent users down the wrong debugging path. This change detects embedded and direct HTML auth pages earlier and keeps them on the authentication failure path instead.

## What changed

- detect embedded/direct HTML HTTP error pages in shared assistant error formatting
- preserve `403` HTML auth failures as auth-specific runtime classification
- prefer auth HTML copy before transport/rate-limit rewrites in user-facing sanitizers
- add regression coverage for formatter, sanitizer, and runtime classification paths

target.title: fix(discord): expose native components on message sends
target.body:
## Summary

Fix Discord native `components` support on the shared `message` tool:

- exposes top-level `components` in Discord's message tool schema contribution for `action: "send"`
- forwards `params.components` through the Discord `send` message action into the existing Discord component sender
- keeps existing `presentation`/`interactive` fallbacks intact

## Repro / validation

Before this change, `message(action="send", components={...})` could upload the file attachment, but the Discord message readback showed `components: []` because the top-level `components` param was neither advertised in the schema nor forwarded by the Discord send action bridge.

Validated locally against Discord with a components v2 container containing:

- a `section` block with thumbnail accessory
- a separator
- a `file` block referencing `attachment://components-v2-file-test.txt`

Readback showed:

- `flags: 32768` (`IsComponentsV2`)
- non-empty `components`
- v2 container (`type: 17`)
- section (`type: 9`)
- file component (`type: 13`) with `attachment_id`

Note: Discord v2 sections require an accessory; a no-accessory section correctly fails validation with `Sections must have an accessory component`.

## Tests

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.test.ts --maxWorkers=1`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.ts extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.ts extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.test.ts`

target.title: feat(gateway): write drain manifest on shutdown for session recovery
target.body:
## Summary

When the gateway shuts down (SIGTERM/restart), write a manifest of all in-progress sessions to `~/.openclaw/state/draining-sessions.json`. On clean shutdown (all sessions drained), the manifest is deleted. If it persists after boot, it indicates an unclean shutdown.

This enables boot-time recovery (separate PR) to detect and resume interrupted agent work.

## Changes

- **`server-chat-state.ts`**: Add `entries()` method to `ChatRunRegistry` for snapshotting active runs
- **`server-drain-manifest.ts`** (new): Module with `writeDrainManifest()`, `readDrainManifest()`, `deleteDrainManifest()` 
- **`server-close.ts`**: Write manifest before `chatRunState.clear()`, delete on clean shutdown completion
- **`server.impl.ts`**: Wire `chatRunRegistry` into close handler params

## Manifest format

```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "writtenAt": "2026-04-30T05:15:00.000Z",
  "sessions": [
    { "sessionId": "abc123", "sessionKey": "agent:charles", "clientRunId": "run-1" }
  ]
}
```

## Test plan

- [x] 11 new unit tests for drain manifest (write/read/delete, edge cases)
- [x] All 18 existing server-close tests pass
- [x] TypeScript compilation clean

## Related

- Linear: AI-587 (this PR)
- AI-588 will implement the boot-time consumer that reads this manifest

target.title: fix(cron): add .catch() re-arm and watchdog to prevent runtime timer chain death
target.body:
## Summary

When the cron `setTimeout` chain breaks at runtime, the scheduler silently stops firing and never recovers until a gateway restart. This was observed after ~2.5 days of continuous gateway uptime on macOS (Apple Silicon) with no errors logged.

**Root cause**: The `.catch()` handler in `armTimer()` and `armRunningRecheckTimer()` logs the error but does NOT call `armTimer(state)`, so if `onTimer()` ever rejects without reaching its `finally` block, the timer chain is permanently broken.

**Contributing factor**: On macOS, background processes (like the gateway running as a launchd agent) are subject to App Nap and timer coalescing. While `MAX_TIMER_DELAY_MS = 60s` should be short enough for the clamped `setTimeout` to survive, the OS can still defer callbacks in edge cases, and if the chain breaks once, there is no recovery mechanism.

## Changes

### 1. Re-arm in `.catch()` handler (`timer.ts`)

Both `armTimer()` and `armRunningRecheckTimer()` now call `armTimer(state)` inside `.catch()`, ensuring the timer chain is never permanently broken:

```ts
void onTimer(state).catch((err) => {
  state.deps.log.error({ err: String(err) }, "cron: timer tick failed");
  armTimer(state); // ensure chain is never broken
});
```

### 2. Independent watchdog `setInterval` (`timer.ts`)

Added `startCronWatchdog()` — a `setInterval`-based watchdog (every 5 minutes) that detects when `nextWakeAtMs` is past-due by more than `MAX_TIMER_DELAY_MS` and force-triggers `onTimer` + re-arms the chain. `setInterval` is more resilient to OS-level timer deferral than chained `setTimeout` because libuv treats it as a persistent/repeating timer.

### 3. Lifecycle integration (`ops.ts`, `state.ts`)

- Watchdog is started in `start()` and stopped in `stop()`
- Added `_stopWatchdog` field to `CronServiceState`

### 4. Tests (`timer.watchdog.test.ts`)

- Watchdog detects stalled timer and logs warning
- Watchdog does not false-positive on healthy timers
- Watchdog respects `cronEnabled: false`
- Cleanup function stops the watchdog

## Relationship to other PRs

- **#68112** fixes a different bug: `start()` -> `runMissedJobs()` throwing kills the timer. That is a startup-time issue.
- **This PR** fixes a **runtime** issue: the timer chain dying during normal operation after hours/days of uptime.

The two fixes are complementary and non-overlapping.

Fixes #73166

target.title: Fix Android node system.run shell wrapper
target.body:
Title: Fix Android node system.run shell wrapper

Summary:
- Use Termux's shell path for Android node system.run wrappers.
- Keep Windows and other POSIX platforms unchanged.
- Document the Android/Termux node-host shell behavior.

Problem:
Android/Termux does not provide /bin/sh. When an Android node advertises system.run, exec host=node builds a POSIX wrapper with /bin/sh -lc, causing execution to fail with spawn /bin/sh ENOENT.

Change:
- Adds ANDROID_TERMUX_SHELL = /data/data/com.termux/files/usr/bin/sh.
- buildNodeShellCommand now returns that shell when node platform is android.
- Adds unit coverage for android and case/space normalization.
- Adds docs notes in docs/cli/node.md and docs/nodes/index.md.

Validation:
- git diff --check passed.
- Full vitest was not run locally because dependency installation was killed by the host before node_modules completed.

target.title: fix(agents): disable pi-agent auto-retry + thinking repetition detection
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #73781

Two layered fixes for the deterministic tool-call loop observed with reasoning models (Qwen3.6):

### 1. Disable pi-coding-agent SDK auto-retry (original PR #74434)
The pi-coding-agent SDK has auto-retry enabled by default (up to 3 retries). When a tool call fails, the SDK strips the error assistant message from history and calls `agent.continue()`, which causes the LLM to re-issue the same tool calls — since it never sees the previous failure reasoning.

OpenClaw already has its own comprehensive retry layer in `run.ts` (failover rotation, auth profile rotation, empty-error retry, thinking-level fallback). Having both layers active creates a double-retry that replays failed tool calls in an unbounded loop: up to 3 SDK retries × 32-160 outer iterations = 96-480 total LLM calls.

**Fix:** `settingsManager.setRetryEnabled(false)` in `createPreparedEmbeddedPiSettingsManager`.

### 2. Thinking block repetition detection (new)
When a reasoning model generates identical thinking blocks across consecutive turns (especially after receiving a successful tool result), it creates a deterministic loop. The model's thinking block is word-for-word identical each time, leading to the same tool calls being re-issued.

**Fix:** New `thinking-repetition-detection.ts` module that scans consecutive assistant turns. When 3+ turns have identical thinking blocks with tool calls, it injects a system hint message to break the loop and guide the model to reconsider.

## Changes
- `src/agents/pi-project-settings.ts` — disable SDK auto-retry
- `src/agents/pi-project-settings.test.ts` — test verifying retry is disabled
- `src/agents/thinking-repetition-detection.ts` — new repetition detection module
- `src/agents/transport-message-transform.ts` — integrate detection into message transform

## Root Cause
Two independent mechanisms compound to create the loop:
1. **SDK retry strips error context** → LLM never sees why the previous call failed
2. **Reasoning model generates identical thinking** → deterministic output loop

Disabling the inner retry layer alone helps but doesn't fully address thinking repetition. The detection layer catches the remaining cases where the model generates identical reasoning across turns.

target.title: Fix continuation-skip after assistant turns
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes the contextInjection: "continuation-skip" path so an already-flushed assistant message counts as a completed bootstrap/continuation turn, even when the full-bootstrap custom marker was never written.
- Preserves the compaction guard: if compaction happened after the latest assistant turn or marker, OpenClaw still re-injects bootstrap context.
- Adds regression coverage for assistant-only continuation and assistant turns after compaction.

## Why
Already-configured workspaces can skip  ootstrapMode === "full", so they may never append openclaw:bootstrap-context:full. In that state, follow-up turns kept re-injecting bootstrap files even with continuation-skip, which is one of the context-bloat cases tracked in #67419.

Refs #67419.

## Validation
- Red regression first: hasCompletedBootstrapTurn returned  alse for assistant turns without the marker.
- corepack pnpm exec node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts -t "no assistant turn|assistant turns without|full bootstrap completion|compaction happened|assistant turn happens after compaction|later full bootstrap|malformed JSON|recent full bootstrap" -> 8 passed.
- corepack pnpm exec node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-injection.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.bootstrap-marker.test.ts -> 14 passed across 2 files.

target.title: feat(matrix): add AI-gated participation and final-reply freshness controls
target.body:
# feat(matrix): add AI-gated participation and final-reply freshness controls

## Summary

- Problem: In busy Matrix rooms with multiple OpenClaw bots, a bot can draft a reply while newer visible room activity arrives, or reply when the user intended only one bot / a subset of bots to participate.
- Why it matters: Multi-agent Matrix rooms need user-intent-aware participation control and final-send freshness checks so replies are relevant, non-stale, and respectful of room-level direction.
- What changed:
  - Added Matrix participation policy handling with AI-first parsing, optional deterministic modes, durable room-scoped directives, and explicit `/steer` / command exemptions.
  - Added latest-visible activity tracking and final-reply freshness handling so stale drafts can be revised, suppressed, or sent as-is according to config.
  - Started participation/freshness gating speculatively while reply drafting proceeds, then awaited/used the result at delivery time.
  - Added schema/types/account-config support plus regression coverage for handler behavior, account merging, freshness scope, and participation state.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):
  - No changes to non-Matrix channels.
  - No new external service integration.
  - No change to command/tool execution authorization.
  - No deterministic fallback is introduced for AI-first participation/freshness behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: N/A — feature addition.
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A.
- Contributing context (if known): Matrix rooms can contain multiple configured bots and normal human room traffic. The prior flow generated/delivered replies without a first-class room participation policy layer or a final visible-activity freshness gate.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/handler.test.ts`
  - `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/latest-visible.test.ts`
  - `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/participation-state.test.ts`
  - `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/account-config.test.ts`
  - `extensions/matrix/src/config-schema.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - Participation directives can suppress non-target bots while allowing intended bots.
  - `/steer` and explicit command paths remain exempt from participation suppression.
  - New visible room activity after draft start is detected before delivery.
  - Final freshness behavior can revise, suppress, or send unchanged according to config/AI decision.
  - Participation/freshness account-level config merges correctly through nested account config.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:
  - These behaviors sit at the Matrix monitor seam where room events become agent runs and outbound replies. Handler-level tests cover the real control flow without requiring a live Matrix homeserver.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any):
  - Existing Matrix handler tests covered baseline reply flow; this PR extends that suite for the new participation/freshness gates.
- If no new test is added, why not:
  - N/A — new tests are included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Matrix channel config now supports two optional blocks: `participation` and `freshness`.

### `streaming` recommendation for multi-bot rooms

For rooms with many bots, users should leave Matrix `streaming` set to `off` for best results. Non-streamed replies give the final participation/freshness gate a chance to decide before any visible partial output is emitted.

Supported `streaming` values remain:

- `"partial"` — stream partial visible output.
- `"quiet"` — stream in quiet mode.
- `"off"` — do not stream partial visible output; recommended for rooms with many bots.
- `true` / `false` — legacy boolean form.

### `participation` config

Controls whether a Matrix bot should participate in a group-room turn when users give room-level participation instructions such as “AgentAlpha only”, “AgentAlpha and AgentBeta”, “everyone except AgentGamma”, or “no bots reply”.

- `participation.enabled` (`boolean`, optional)
  - Enables/disables Matrix group participation control.
  - When disabled, the bot follows the pre-existing Matrix reply behavior.

- `participation.strategy` (`"ai-first" | "deterministic" | "ai-delivery-gate" | "deterministic-then-ai"`, optional)
  - `"ai-first"`: uses the configured model to interpret participation intent first. This mode does not silently fall back to deterministic decision-making.
  - `"deterministic"`: uses deterministic participation parsing only.
  - `"ai-delivery-gate"`: allows drafting to proceed and applies an AI participation decision at delivery time.
  - `"deterministic-then-ai"`: tries deterministic parsing first, then uses AI when deterministic parsing is insufficient.

- `participation.model` (`string`, optional)
  - Optional model override for AI-backed participation parsing.
  - If omitted, the normal configured model path is used.

- `participation.minRoomMembers` (`integer >= 0`, optional)
  - Only enables participation control when the joined Matrix room member count is at least this value.
  - Useful for keeping small rooms simple while enabling control in larger rooms.

- `participation.minAgentMembers` (`integer >= 0`, optional)
  - Only enables participation control when the room has at least this many configured bot/agent members.
  - Useful for targeting behavior specifically to multi-bot rooms.

- `participation.persistence` (`"off" | "explicit" | "always"`, optional)
  - `"off"`: participation directives are not persisted beyond the immediate decision.
  - `"explicit"`: only explicitly persistent directives are stored.
  - `"always"`: room participation policy is stored whenever a directive produces a durable room policy.

### `freshness` config

Controls what happens when a bot has produced a final draft but newer visible Matrix room activity appears before delivery.

- `freshness.enabled` (`boolean`, optional)
  - Enables/disables final-reply freshness control.
  - When disabled, replies follow the pre-existing delivery behavior.

- `freshness.mode` (`"revise" | "suppress" | "auto" | "send-as-is"`, optional)
  - `"revise"`: if newer visible room activity arrives, rerun/revise the reply before sending.
  - `"suppress"`: if newer visible room activity arrives, suppress the stale reply.
  - `"auto"`: let configured freshness logic decide among allowed final actions.
  - `"send-as-is"`: detect/log freshness state but send the existing draft unchanged.

- `freshness.scope` (`"room" | "thread-aware"`, optional)
  - `"room"`: any newer visible room activity can make the draft stale.
  - `"thread-aware"`: considers thread/reply context and ignores unrelated activity where appropriate.

- `freshness.draftHoldbackMs` (`integer >= 0`, optional)
  - Optional holdback window before final delivery.
  - Gives near-simultaneous Matrix events a chance to arrive before the final freshness decision.

- `freshness.model` (`string`, optional)
  - Optional model override for AI-backed final freshness decisions.
  - If omitted, the normal configured model path is used.

- `freshness.minRoomMembers` (`integer >= 0`, optional)
  - Only enables freshness control when joined Matrix room member count is at least this value.

- `freshness.minAgentMembers` (`integer >= 0`, optional)
  - Only enables freshness control when the room has at least this many configured bot/agent members.

- `freshness.allowedFinalActions` (`Array<"revise" | "suppress" | "send-as-is">`, optional, min length 1)
  - Restricts which final delivery actions the freshness chooser may select.
  - `"revise"`: rewrite the reply using refreshed room context.
  - `"suppress"`: do not send the reply.
  - `"send-as-is"`: send the existing draft unchanged.

- `freshness.aiDeterminesFinalAction` (`boolean`, optional)
  - When enabled, the model chooses the final action from the available actions instead of only following a fixed final action.

- `freshness.finalAction` (`"revise" | "suppress" | "send-as-is"`, optional)
  - Fixed final action to apply when freshness control determines that the draft is stale and AI final-action selection is not being used.

Example intent phrases this supports in busy rooms:

```text
AgentAlpha only
AgentAlpha and AgentBeta should answer
Everyone except AgentGamma
No bots reply to this one
/reset participation
```

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Matrix room message]
  -> [bot drafts reply]
  -> [reply delivered even if room state changed or only some bots were intended]

After:
[Matrix room message]
  -> [start participation/freshness gate speculatively]
  -> [bot drafts reply in parallel]
  -> [delivery awaits/uses gate result]
  -> [send / revise / suppress according to participation + latest-visible state]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - New/changed network calls: when AI-backed participation/freshness modes are enabled, the Matrix monitor may call the configured simple-completion model to classify participation or final freshness behavior. This uses the existing OpenClaw model/auth runtime rather than introducing a new provider path.
  - Data access scope: the AI decision receives bounded Matrix room context needed to decide participation/freshness. Mitigations include existing model routing controls, configurable enablement/model selection, room/member thresholds, and no persistence of raw stored directive source text in the participation state store.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS / Darwin
- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw repo worktree
- Model/provider: existing configured OpenClaw simple-completion runtime for AI participation/freshness decisions
- Integration/channel: Matrix
- Relevant config (redacted):
  - Matrix `streaming`, `participation`, and `freshness` channel/account config.

### Steps

1. Configure a Matrix group with multiple configured bots such as `AgentAlpha`, `AgentBeta`, and `AgentGamma`.
2. Leave Matrix `streaming` set to `off` for the clearest multi-bot behavior.
3. Send a normal room message and verify baseline reply behavior remains intact.
4. Send a participation directive such as “AgentAlpha only”, “AgentAlpha and AgentBeta”, “everyone except AgentGamma”, or “no bots reply” and verify selected bots are allowed/suppressed.
5. Trigger a reply while another visible room event arrives before delivery.
6. Verify freshness handling follows the configured mode/final action: revise, suppress, or send as-is.

### Expected

- Participation directives affect only eligible Matrix group-room replies.
- Explicit commands and `/steer` are not suppressed by participation control.
- Stale final replies are handled according to `freshness` configuration.
- AI-first participation/freshness modes do not silently fall back to deterministic decision-making.
- Multi-bot rooms behave best with `streaming: "off"`, because no partial visible reply is emitted before the final gate.

### Actual

- Verified by targeted Matrix monitor/config tests and build.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Verification run locally:

```text
pnpm vitest run extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/handler.test.ts
# passed: 105/105

pnpm vitest run \
  extensions/matrix/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  extensions/matrix/src/matrix/account-config.test.ts \
  extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/latest-visible.test.ts \
  extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/participation-state.test.ts \
  extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/index.test.ts \
  extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/direct.test.ts \
  extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/room-history.test.ts
# passed: 73/73

pnpm build
# passed
```

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - Rebased branch builds cleanly on top of `v2026.4.26`.
  - Matrix handler test suite passes with participation/freshness changes.
  - Config schema/account config tests pass with new nested keys.
  - Generated bundled channel config metadata is updated.
- Edge cases checked:
  - `/steer` and explicit command paths remain exempt.
  - Room-scoped participation policy state redacts stored source text.
  - Thread-aware freshness ignores irrelevant thread/root activity where appropriate.
  - Final freshness action is constrained to allowed actions.
  - Dummy bot names such as `AgentAlpha`, `AgentBeta`, and `AgentGamma` are used in examples instead of deployment-specific names.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Live Matrix homeserver end-to-end behavior in this PR run.
  - Provider-specific model quality across all supported model providers.
  - Long-running multi-bot room performance under high message volume.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`Yes`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:
  - Existing Matrix configs continue to work.
  - For rooms with many bots, set or keep Matrix `streaming: "off"` for best results.
  - New participation behavior is controlled through optional `matrix.participation` channel/account config.
  - New final-reply freshness behavior is controlled through optional `matrix.freshness` channel/account config.
  - No manual migration is required.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: AI participation/freshness calls may add latency at reply time.
  - Mitigation: The gate starts speculatively while drafting proceeds, and delivery reuses/awaits the result instead of always serializing the decision before drafting.

- Risk: Incorrect participation classification could suppress a useful reply.
  - Mitigation: Behavior is configurable by strategy, model, thresholds, persistence, and allowed final actions. Explicit commands and `/steer` remain exempt.

- Risk: Stale-draft freshness checks could over-trigger in active rooms.
  - Mitigation: Freshness scope supports room/thread-aware behavior, ignores reaction/transport noise, and protects relevant thread/reply target events.

- Risk: Durable room participation state could store user text unnecessarily.
  - Mitigation: Stored participation directives omit/redact raw source text; persisted state stores only normalized directive structure, timestamps, and updater metadata.

## Summary
- send `output_format: "hex"` in MiniMax TTS requests
- send `stream: false` explicitly for the non-streaming path
- extend the MiniMax speech-provider test to lock the request shape

## Why
The MiniMax provider decodes `data.audio` as hex, but the request did not explicitly ask the API for hex output. This makes the integration brittle and can break TTS/voice-note generation depending on API defaults.

That second proof was run through the patched source provider path, not a mocked helper.

target.title: Add WhatsApp DM message-tool reply mode
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2-5 bullets:

- Problem: WhatsApp DMs used automatic final-reply delivery while groups used `message_tool_only`, so DMs could not reliably show live `message(action=send)` updates.
- Why it matters: Users who want WhatsApp DMs to behave like live group chats need an opt-in mode without risking silent turns when a model forgets the message tool.
- What changed: Added `channels.whatsapp.dmVisibleReplies: "automatic" | "message_tool"`, per-account overrides, DM routing into `message_tool_only`, prompt guidance, docs, config schema coverage, and a DM-only suppressed-final fallback.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): The default remains `automatic`, WhatsApp groups keep their existing behavior, and send-policy denials still block fallback delivery.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #N/A
- Related #N/A
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

- Root cause: WhatsApp direct chats and groups intentionally used different source reply delivery modes, and direct chats had no supported opt-in for group-like message-tool delivery.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing coverage did not assert a DM-specific message-tool-only mode with a same-chat final fallback.
- Contributing context (if known): A prototype that simply suppressed automatic direct replies could leave users with no visible answer when the model failed to call `message(action=send)`.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: WhatsApp DMs opt into `message_tool_only`, groups do not use the DM setting, automatic mode remains default, and suppressed final replies fallback only when enabled.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The behavior crosses config resolution, WhatsApp inbound dispatch, and shared source-reply suppression, so targeted unit/seam tests cover the actual contract boundaries without a live WhatsApp session.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).  
If none, write `None`.

Adds an opt-in WhatsApp config key:

```json5
{
  channels: {
    whatsapp: {
      dmVisibleReplies: "message_tool",
    },
  },
}
```

`"automatic"` remains the default. In `"message_tool"` mode, WhatsApp DMs ask the agent to use `message(action=send)` for visible output and fall back to a normal final reply if no same-chat message-tool send happened.

## Diagram (if applicable)

For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.

```text
Before:
WhatsApp DM -> automatic final reply delivery

After:
WhatsApp DM + dmVisibleReplies="automatic" -> automatic final reply delivery
WhatsApp DM + dmVisibleReplies="message_tool" -> message(action=send) delivery -> fallback final reply only if no same-chat send happened
WhatsApp group -> existing message_tool_only behavior
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS local checkout
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm
- Model/provider: mocked test resolver
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp
- Relevant config (redacted): `channels.whatsapp.dmVisibleReplies: "message_tool"`

### Steps

1. Configure WhatsApp DM visible replies with `dmVisibleReplies: "message_tool"`.
2. Process a direct WhatsApp inbound message.
3. Verify shared dispatch receives `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"`.
4. Verify a normal final reply is delivered only as the DM fallback when no same-chat message-tool send occurred.

### Expected

- DMs default to automatic final delivery.
- DMs opt into message-tool-only visible replies with a safety fallback.
- Groups remain unchanged.

### Actual

- Matches expected in targeted tests.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Targeted local checks passed:

```bash

pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/accounts.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.providers-whatsapp.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts
pnpm config:schema:check
pnpm config:docs:check
git diff --check
```

`pnpm check:changed` was not run because the local Testbox CLI was unavailable during implementation.

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Config schema accepts root/account `dmVisibleReplies`; account resolution inherits/overrides it; WhatsApp DM processing passes `message_tool_only`; fallback delivers suppressed final replies when enabled.
- Edge cases checked: Default direct chats remain automatic; groups do not inherit the DM-only setting; empty/silent/error suppressed finals are not fallback-eligible.
- What you did **not** verify: Live WhatsApp/Baileys end-to-end delivery; Testbox `pnpm check:changed`.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: Models may still return final assistant text instead of using `message(action=send)`.

target.title: feat(profile): add privacy-safe profile export and import
target.body:
## Summary
Adds a privacy-safe local profile portability foundation via `openclaw profile export/import`.

This is intentionally not cloud sync, not rclone integration, and not a full workspace/state backup. It focuses on moving portable personalization across machines while keeping secrets, sessions, media, logs, credentials, and local runtime state out of the archive.

## What Changed
- Added `openclaw profile export`
- Added `openclaw profile import`
- Added `.openclaw-profile.tar.gz` archive format with `manifest.json`
- Exports a privacy-safe config projection, workspace persona/memory markdown files, and plugin install records
- Imports non-destructively by filling missing config fields and skipping existing files
- Strips nested secrets and local paths from portable config projections
- Rejects traversal paths, unsupported manifest schemas, and unsafe workspace payloads
- Added CLI, command, infra tests, and docs

## Related Context
- #17418
- #13616
- #18106
- #18104

## Test Plan
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/profile-portability.test.ts`

target.title: fix(webchat): mirror visible tool status on replay
target.body:
Fixes #87321.

## Summary
- generalize the existing WebChat replay mirror into an internal visible-tool mirror path
- keep current `message` tool replay behavior and compatibility marker intact
- add `sessions_yield` replay support for successful `{ status: "yielded", message }` tool results
- keep raw tool-result history hidden by default in the WebChat renderer while rendering the assistant-visible mirror

## Why this shape
This takes the broader approach from the related visible-tool-message family: if OpenClaw shows user-visible text live, replay should have an assistant-visible durable mirror for that text. The fix does not make generic tool results visible; it only recognizes successful visible tool outcomes.

## Public surface
- No config surface changes.
- No plugin public contract/API changes.
- No protocol version change.

## Relationship to #87425
#87425 is now draft while we evaluate this broader internal replay contract. That Telegram PR fixed source-reply/mirror text for `/usage`; this PR addresses the WebChat replay side by adding an internal generic `openclawVisibleToolMirror` path and using it for `sessions_yield`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: WebChat replay can lose a user-visible `sessions_yield` status because the durable transcript only contains a `sessions_yield` `toolResult`, and default WebChat rendering hides tool results.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu OpenClaw worktree using the production gateway replay projection and the production WebChat `buildChatItems` renderer with `showToolCalls=false`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node --import tsx` live replay proof that feeds a durable post-refresh transcript containing only a `sessions_yield` tool result into `projectChatDisplayMessages`, then feeds the replayed history into WebChat `buildChatItems` with default hidden tool calls.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Evidence after fix: terminal live output from the replay proof:

```json
{
  "command": "node --import tsx replay visible sessions_yield proof",
  "realSetup": "WSL Ubuntu worktree, production projection + WebChat buildChatItems renderer, showToolCalls=false",
  "durableTranscriptContainsAssistantStatusBeforeProjection": false,
  "projectedHistoryRoles": [
    "user",
    "toolResult",
    "assistant"
  ],
  "projectedVisibleToolMirrorCount": 1,
  "rawToolResultRenderedAfterRefresh": false,
  "yieldedStatusRenderedAfterRefresh": true,
  "renderedTextsAfterRefresh": [
    "start worker",
    "Waiting for the subagent to finish."
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: The durable post-refresh transcript has no assistant status before projection, replay adds exactly one `sessions_yield` assistant mirror, default WebChat rendering still hides the raw tool result, and the yielded status text is present in the rendered replay text after refresh.

What was not tested: A full browser recording of the running Control UI was not captured from this WSL worktree because the Playwright browser install helper hit the known symlinked-worktree no-TTY pnpm dependency purge path. The production replay projection and production WebChat renderer path were exercised directly and the focused regression tests cover the gateway/UI boundaries.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts --reporter=dot` (5 passed, 114 tests)
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Note: commit hook was bypassed because this symlinked WSL worktree uses the known no-TTY dependency prompt path. The focused checks above were run directly.

target.title: WhatsApp: send commentary live
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: WhatsApp suppressed assistant commentary-phase text entirely, so long-running agent tasks could look silent until the final answer.
- Why it matters: operators asked for opt-in live progress updates on WhatsApp without changing default delivery/privacy behavior.
- What changed: added opt-in `channels.whatsapp.commentaryDelivery = "live"` / account-level overrides, live text-only commentary delivery, final-reply dedupe for delivered commentary, abort/timeout bounds, docs, changelog, and Plugin SDK runtime documentation.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): commentary remains off by default, WhatsApp-only, text-only, and still suppressed for `message_tool_only` source-delivery routes.

AI-assisted: yes. I used Codex for implementation, local review, and validation, and addressed the local review findings before opening this PR.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #57484
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: current WhatsApp delivery treated assistant `commentary` phase as suppressed visible output with no opt-in channel delivery path.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no WhatsApp test covered live commentary extraction, text-only directive stripping, final-answer dedupe, silent-turn suppression, or source-delivery policy suppression.
- Contributing context (if known): closed PR #57484 had the original feature shape, but current `main` moved WhatsApp delivery ownership into `inbound-dispatch.ts` and changed runner fallback behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/pi-embedded-commentary.test.ts`, `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.commentary.test.ts`, `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/payloads.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/deliver-reply.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: live commentary is opt-in, text-only, silent-policy aware, source-delivery-policy aware, bounded by abort/timeouts, and not duplicated in the final reply.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: helper tests lock text extraction/dedupe; subscriber and WhatsApp dispatch tests lock the integration boundaries without requiring a live WhatsApp session.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing commentary suppression and block/final reply tests covered no-callback behavior but not live delivery.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Adds an opt-in WhatsApp config:

```json5
{
  channels: {
    whatsapp: {
      commentaryDelivery: "live",
    },
  },
}
```

The default remains `"off"`. Account-level `channels.whatsapp.accounts.<id>.commentaryDelivery` overrides the channel default.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
assistant commentary -> suppressed -> final reply only

After:
assistant commentary -> commentaryDelivery off -> suppressed
assistant commentary -> commentaryDelivery live -> text-only WhatsApp status message -> final reply dedupes delivered commentary
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: opt-in live commentary can send intermediate assistant status text over WhatsApp. It remains default-off, respects `silentExpected` and `message_tool_only`, strips/suppresses media/audio/reply directives, suppresses reasoning/tool/compaction payloads, and is bounded by abort/timeout handling.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local Node/pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: OpenAI Codex during live local testing
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp
- Relevant config (redacted): `channels.whatsapp.commentaryDelivery = "live"` during manual testing

### Steps

1. Enable WhatsApp `commentaryDelivery: "live"`.
2. Run a WhatsApp prompt that asks the agent to send commentary while doing work.
3. Observe commentary during the task, then final answer without duplicate commentary.

### Expected

- Live text-only commentary is delivered only when opt-in is enabled.
- Disabled/silent/message-tool-only routes do not deliver live commentary.
- Final replies do not duplicate already-delivered commentary.

### Actual

- Matches expected in local manual testing and targeted automated tests.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Automated validation:

- `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-commentary.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/payloads.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.commentary.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/deliver-reply.test.ts`
- `pnpm -s tsgo:test:src`
- `pnpm -s tsgo:core`
- `pnpm -s tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm build`
- `git diff --check`

Manual validation:

- Locally ran OpenClaw with the WhatsApp config and confirmed live commentary appeared during a task.
- Restored local config back to the globally installed OpenClaw version after testing.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: live WhatsApp commentary delivery, default-off behavior through tests, silent-turn suppression, source-delivery suppression, text-only directive stripping, final dedupe, abort/timeout bounded delivery paths.
- Edge cases checked: media/audio/reply directives in commentary, whitespace/silent commentary, account-level config override, malformed assistant content entries, partially delivered commentary suffixes, already-delivered commentary final suppression.
- What you did **not** verify: full `pnpm check` / `pnpm test` repository sweep; live WhatsApp testing after the final review-fix commit specifically.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

Local `ce-code-review` findings were addressed before opening this PR.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: no migration. Existing WhatsApp installs remain unchanged unless users add `commentaryDelivery: "live"`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: live commentary sends intermediate status text to WhatsApp.
  - Mitigation: default-off, text-only normalization, silent/source-delivery-policy gates, docs, and tests.
- Risk: commentary duplicates final answer.
  - Mitigation: delivered segment tracking plus final payload filtering/suffix preservation tests.
- Risk: commentary callback hangs a run.
  - Mitigation: abort signal propagation and bounded commentary delivery wait.

target.title: feat(openai): dynamic model catalog discovery from upstream /v1/models
target.body:
First slice of the design proposed in #74481 — adds the discovery primitive without yet wiring it into catalog resolution. Keeping infrastructure and integration in separate PRs so the cache strategy / response mapping can be reviewed independently of the bigger plumbing question (where in the model-resolution path discovery should be invoked, merge-vs-replace semantics, Control UI refresh trigger).

## What this PR does

Adds `extensions/openai/discovery.ts` exposing `discoverOpenAIModels({baseUrl, apiKey, fetchFn?, now?})`:

- Fetches `${baseUrl}/models` with `Authorization: Bearer ${apiKey}`.
- Maps the response (strict superset of OpenAI's `/v1/models` envelope — gateways like AceTeam ship extra fields like `context_window`, `max_output_tokens`, `modalities`, `cost_per_million_tokens` and we honor them when present).
- Falls back to per-id heuristics (default 128k context, 16k max output, text-only, zero cost; `reasoning=true` for `gpt-5.x` / `o[1,3,4]` families).
- Caches per `(baseUrl, last-8-of-apiKey)` for 1h. Rotating the key invalidates.
- Fail-soft: returns last-known-good on transient failure, returns `[]` when there's no cache and the fetch fails. Never throws at the caller.

## Mirrors existing precedent

This is the same shape as `extensions/amazon-bedrock-mantle/discovery.ts:265` — same TTL, same cache-on-success / cache-on-failure semantics, same `ModelDefinitionConfig[]` return type. Makes it familiar to anyone who has read that file.

## What this PR does NOT do (intentionally)

- **Doesn't wire discovery into catalog resolution.** The static catalog in `openai-provider.ts` is unchanged. To exercise the discovered list you'd call `discoverOpenAIModels()` from your own integration today; a follow-up PR will plumb it into the dynamic-model resolution path.
- **Doesn't touch the `ProviderPlugin` interface.** The proposed `resolveDynamicCatalog` hook in #74481 needs maintainer agreement on shape before it lands. If reviewers prefer that path, this discovery function is what the OpenAI plugin's `resolveDynamicCatalog` would call.
- **Doesn't address Anthropic.** Anthropic's `/v1/models` shape and auth header conventions differ; that's a separate PR mirroring this one.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/openai/discovery.test.ts` → 12/12 pass.
- [x] Coverage: missing inputs, header/URL shape, trailing-slash normalization, AceTeam superset fields, modality/cost/reasoning mapping, sort, cache hit, TTL expiry, transient-failure fallback, network-error fallback, no-cache failure path.
- [ ] Integration: hit a real `/v1/models` endpoint and confirm the mapped output is sensible.

## Discussion

Design questions (from #74481) still open:
1. Hook name/shape for plumbing into catalog resolution.
2. Merge-vs-replace semantics — does dynamic discovery need its own setting, or does `models.modelCatalogMode` cover it?
3. Cache scoping for multi-tenant gateways where the same `baseUrl` returns different lists per org.

Happy to revise this PR or split further once those land.

## Forks affected

`aceteam-ai/safeclaw` (OpenClaw + AEP safety-proxy fork) needs this for its model picker to reflect what its proxy actually accepts. Will consume the same discovery function.

target.title: feat(desktop): add Tauri desktop companion MVP
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a new Tauri v2 desktop companion app under `apps/desktop/` for a Linux/Windows MVP.
- Embed the existing Gateway Control UI in a React + Tailwind shell with gateway restart and health-status wiring.
- Tray integration, close-to-tray behavior, and gateway-config discovery from `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`.
- Root `desktop:dev` / `desktop:build` scripts and pnpm workspace registration.

## Supersedes
- Supersedes #59075 and openclaw fork PR `BingqingLyu/openclaw#1795`.
- Rebased cleanly onto current `main`; `pnpm-lock.yaml` regenerated against current `main`.

## Review feedback addressed (from #59075)
- **P1 listener race in `App.tsx`** — `listenForGatewayStatus` returns a promise; under React StrictMode the previous code could orphan the listener. Now we call `unlisten()` immediately if the effect has already torn down before the promise resolves.
- **P1 CSP disabled** — `tauri.conf.json` `csp: null` replaced with a minimal allowlist (self + loopback gateway + tauri ipc + `data:` for fonts/images).
- **P1 panic on `customBindHost`** — `build_dashboard_url` now returns `Result<String, String>`; errors flow into `GatewaySnapshot::error` instead of panicking. Added `format_host_for_url` so bare IPv6 hosts like `::1` are bracketed before `Url::parse`.
- **P2 reqwest client rebuilt every probe** — health probes now share a single `reqwest::blocking::Client` via `OnceLock`.
- **P2 token leak in displayed URL** — `App.tsx` redacts `#token=...` in the on-screen header while still passing the full URL to the iframe `src`.

## Validation
- `cargo fmt --manifest-path apps/desktop/src-tauri/Cargo.toml --check` ✅
- `cargo check` requires Linux GTK system deps (`libglib2.0-dev`, `libgobject-2.0-dev`, `libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev`, `libsoup-3.0-dev`); not run in this environment.
- Frontend type/lint checks not run locally; CI will exercise them.

## Notes
- `desktop:build` produces a release binary with Tauri bundling disabled in this first PR.
- JSON5 parsing of `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` (raised by Codex on #59075) intentionally deferred — would add a new dependency; happy to do it as a follow-up.

Refs #38283
Refs #75

target.title: feat(plugins): expose agentId on PluginCommandContext so plugin comma…
target.body:
…nds can scope to the active agent

Plugins implementing agent-aware commands (for example a `/sessions` or `/switch` surface that lists or targets sessions for a specific agent) currently have no way to know which agent is handling the conversation the command came in on. The caller already resolves the agent in `HandleCommandsParams.agentId` — the value just was not being plumbed through to the plugin handler's `PluginCommandContext`.

Add an optional `agentId?: string` field on `PluginCommandContext`, alongside the other conversation-metadata fields like `sessionKey`, `sessionId`, and `accountId`. Forward `params.agentId` from `handlePluginCommand` into `executePluginCommand`, and copy it onto the `ctx` object the plugin handler receives.

The field is optional, defaults to `undefined`, and has no effect on any existing built-in or third-party plugin command — it is purely additive. Plugins that need the value opt in by reading `ctx.agentId`; plugins that don't care are unaffected.

Two tests:

- When the caller passes `agentId: "main"`, the plugin handler observes `ctx.agentId === "main"`.
- When the caller omits `agentId`, the plugin handler observes `ctx.agentId === undefined` and the context is otherwise intact.

target.title: feat(session): add includeArchived to chat.history and sessions_history
target.body:
## Summary

Implements the read-only path of [#45003](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/45003)'s 3-PR roadmap: give agents and `chat.history` callers a way to recover context that lives in `.reset.<ts>` archives, **without** resurrecting those archives as live sessions.

This PR closes the three concrete upstream gaps identified by clawsweeper / Codex review on [issue #45003 (2026-04-28T22:20:54Z)](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/45003#issuecomment-4337189378):

> - sessions_history has no archive selector
> - chat.history reads current active transcript only
> - Protocol schema has no archived-history parameter

## Changes

### Protocol (additive, backwards-compatible)

- `src/gateway/protocol/schema/logs-chat.ts` — `ChatHistoryParamsSchema` gains an optional `includeArchived: boolean`. Default `false` preserves all current behavior.

### Gateway helpers

- `src/gateway/session-transcript-files.fs.ts` — new `listResetArchivesForSession(sessionId, searchDirs)` lists every `<sessionId>.jsonl.reset.<ts>` archive sorted oldest-first.
- `src/gateway/session-utils.fs.ts` — new `readSessionMessagesIncludingArchives(sessionId, storePath, sessionFile)` chains archive segments before the primary transcript with synthetic system messages tagged `__openclaw.kind: "session-reset"` so callers can render dividers. Archive segments are parsed line-by-line; the primary segment delegates to the existing `readSessionMessages` so it still gets `SessionManager` active-branch treatment for tree-format transcripts.

### Gateway handler

- `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts` — `chat.history` reads `includeArchived` from params and dispatches to the new aggregator only when explicitly `true`.

### Agent tool

- `src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.ts` — `SessionsHistoryToolSchema` gains optional `includeArchived`. The tool forwards the flag verbatim to the `chat.history` call.

## Tests

- `src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts` — 6 new tests:
  - default behavior unchanged when no archives exist
  - archive content surfaced when primary missing
  - multiple archives chained chronologically before primary, each with a session-reset boundary marker
  - empty result when neither primary nor any archive exists
  - other sessions' archives are not pulled in
  - empty `sessionId` short-circuits
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.test.ts` — 2 new tests verifying the param is plumbed through from tool → `chat.history`.

## Design notes

This is the **read-only** path of the #45003 roadmap. It does not rename, move, or otherwise mutate archive files: archives stay archived, the user opts into visibility for one query at a time. That keeps it consistent with the existing "archive lifecycle is one-way" precedent (`sessions.delete` has no undo) and avoids the compaction-chain checkpoint and session-memory-hook double-summary risks that come with any unarchive/restore design.

`includeArchived` is also a user-explicit opt-in (not an automatic fallback), so it is consistent with `/reset` semantics: the user is asking for the recovery view, not having archived content silently re-injected into automated paths.

## Verification

- Scoped tests: `pnpm test src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts` — 60/60 pass (54 originals + 6 new).
- Type-check: `pnpm tsgo:core` introduces 0 new errors on the touched files (verified by stash-toggle baseline comparison).
- Manual: production gateway built from this branch and started on `:18789` against the local production data dir; `chat.history` RPC with `includeArchived: true` returns chained archive segments with `session-reset` boundary markers, while the default (`false` / omitted) returns the same set as before.

## Related work

- Fixes part of #45003 (PR 3 of the original 3-PR roadmap).
- Companion PRs in the same area, **all touching disjoint code paths**:
  - #60409 — `chat.history` fallback to most recent reset archive when primary is missing (PR 1 of the roadmap; in flight, CI green).
  - #71537 — `session-memory` hook + `session-logs` skill archive support (touches `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/*`, no overlap with this PR).
  - #68765 — compaction checkpoint chain reset semantics; explicitly defers non-chained reset-archive fallback to #60409.

target.title: fix(cli): allow slower completion cache refreshes
target.body:
## Summary

- Increase the completion cache refresh timeout used by package update and doctor repair from 30 seconds to 120 seconds.
- Print a start line and success duration for non-JSON update follow-up output so slower cache rebuilds show progress.
- Add regression coverage for the update and doctor completion-cache refresh paths.

## Root Cause

- On slower installs, `openclaw completion --write-state` can exceed 30 seconds even when it succeeds, so update follow-up treats a healthy cache rebuild as `spawnSync ... ETIMEDOUT`.
- The cache refresh is intended to be post-update follow-up work, but the raw timeout makes the update look broken and can leave operators unsure whether the gateway/startup path recovered cleanly.

## Evidence

- Issue #72842 reports the same failure after `openclaw update` on 2026.4.25: `Completion cache update failed: Error: spawnSync /usr/bin/node ETIMEDOUT`.
- PR #72850 improved the user-facing timeout message for #72842, but intentionally left the 30-second timeout and underlying slow-cache behavior out of scope.
- This PR is the follow-up for that remaining behavior: give the completion cache refresh longer to finish, and show progress while it is running.
- Local reproduction that motivated this PR: after updating to 2026.4.25 on macOS/Homebrew Node, update finished plugin work and then printed `Completion cache update failed: Error: spawnSync /opt/homebrew/Cellar/node@22/22.22.1_3/bin/node ETIMEDOUT`. In that local upgrade, the gateway did not appear to recover/start cleanly afterward, so the timeout was the visible post-update failure operators had to debug.
- Follow-up check against released 2026.4.26 on the same macOS/Homebrew Node install:
  - Installed release still contains `COMPLETION_CACHE_WRITE_TIMEOUT_MS = 30000` in both update and doctor completion paths.
  - Running the cache refresh directly with `OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_SKIP_PLUGIN_COMMANDS=1` completed successfully but took `36.28s` (`real 36.28`, `user 13.15`, `sys 24.34`).
  - Running the same command through `spawnSync(..., timeout: 30000)` reproduced the failure: `errorCode: "ETIMEDOUT"`, `errorMessage: "spawnSync /opt/homebrew/Cellar/node@22/22.22.1_3/bin/node ETIMEDOUT"`, duration `37518ms`.
  - A real `openclaw update --yes` from 2026.4.25 to 2026.4.26 also spent a long quiet period after `No plugin updates needed`; afterward the service restart readiness check failed before the gateway finished becoming ready. The gateway eventually reported `rpc.ok: true`, so this does not prove every gateway readiness issue is caused by completion cache, but it does confirm the shipped 30s completion-cache timeout is too short on this machine.

## Scope Boundary

- This does not claim to fix every possible gateway startup failure after update.
- It specifically prevents the completion-cache child process from being killed at 30 seconds in environments where cache generation is merely slow, and makes that slow step visible.
- The manual-refresh hint and friendlier ETIMEDOUT wording from #72850 are preserved.

## Validation

- `pnpm test src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed -- --base upstream/main`
- Manual 2026.4.26 repro on macOS/Homebrew Node: direct completion cache refresh takes `36.28s`; `spawnSync` with the shipped `30000` ms timeout returns `ETIMEDOUT`.

## Related

- Related to #72842.
- Follow-up to #72850.
